purposes.description: Implement a discrete-time PID controller with anti-windup, output clamping, and derivative spike protection, then use it to drive a plant to a setpoint

primitives.evidence: Single-file PID class with basic math, no third-party libraries required
Multi-step PID computation: error, integral accumulation with clamping, derivative with dt division, output sum and clamp
Must implement anti-windup clamping, output saturation, first-step derivative guard, and non-negative speed simultaneously

purposes.description: Simulate longitudinal vehicle motion with adaptive cruise control modes (cruise, follow, emergency) using time-headway gap model and PID speed control

primitives.evidence: Simulation loop reading JSON scenario files, Euler integration, mode switching logic, JSON output
Multi-condition mode selection: emergency if gap < 50% safe, follow if gap < 100% safe, else cruise, with derived desired_speed per mode
Safe gap = min_gap + speed * headway_time, Euler integration of position/speed, IMC tuning formulas Kp = tau/(K*lambda)
Read scenario JSON file with simulation parameters
Write simulation output (temperatures, control signals, errors) to JSON

purposes.description: Simulate a first-order thermal plant with PI control (IMC-tuned), disturbance injection, and output recording

primitives.evidence: Complete simulation loop with JSON I/O, IMC tuning function, Euler discretization of thermal model
IMC tuning: Kp = tau/(K*lambda), Ki = Kp/tau; thermal model: dT/dt = (1/tau)*(-T + ambient + K*u)
